name: Mixed Sprout Salad
tagline: Live-sprouted moong + chana with onion, tomato, and lime
highlight: Sprouting multiplies B-vitamins, vitamin C, and bioavailable minerals

hero.alt: Bowl of pale-green moong sprouts mixed with diced tomato, onion, and lemon
hero.caption: sprout salad — raw, crunchy, lemon-bright

healthFacts: Sprouting raises B1, B6, folate, and vitamin C content by 2–10× vs the dry seed.
Phytate breakdown during sprouting boosts iron, zinc, and magnesium absorption.
Raw, low-glycemic, and high-fiber — gentle on insulin and HbA1c.
Lemon provides vitamin C that further unlocks the iron in the sprouts.

steps.title: Steam-blanch the sprouts (optional)
steps.detail: Steam sprouts for 2 minutes if you want them softer or food-safety conscious — raw is fine for healthy adults. Cool fully before mixing.
steps.tip: Steaming kills any surface bacteria but keeps 90% of the sprouting nutrient gain.

steps.title: Chop and combine
steps.detail: Toss sprouts with onion, tomato, cucumber, green chili, and coriander in a wide bowl.
steps.tip: Cut the vegetables fine — same size as the sprouts so each spoonful is balanced.

steps.title: Dress and serve
steps.detail: Squeeze in lemon juice, sprinkle black salt and a pinch of roasted cumin powder. Toss and serve immediately.
steps.tip: Don't dress in advance — vegetables release water and the salad goes limp.
